evidence_excerpt: Two years after the operation, the patient had a normal gait without pain and range of motion comparable to 4 months post-op, but with slightly reduced internal rotation and persistent secondary dysplasia/partially uneven acetabulum.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: Four months after surgery: normal gait, free mobility with slightly reduced internal rotation. At two years: range of motion comparable to 4 months with normal gait; persistent secondary dysplasia on imaging.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: Two years after the operation: 'We found a normal gait without any pain.'

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Four months after the operation, the patient showed normal gait and free mobility of the left hip with only slightly reduced internal rotation.
Two years after the operation, the patient had a normal gait without any pain.

Two years after the operation, hip range of motion was comparable to 4 months post-op and the femoral head remained centrally positioned, although the acetabulum was partially uneven with secondary dysplasia.

notes_on_uncertainty: The exact date of definitive diagnosis is not stated; the timeline is interpreted relative to pre-operative diagnosis and subsequent surgery. Follow-up described at approximately 4 months and about 22–24 months post-op; long-term structural dysplasia persisted but without pain.
